descriptions: Alberta Warriors adopted the Warriors banner from Manitoba Warriors.
edges.evidence_quote: Other organizations that have adopted the 'Warriors' banner such as the Alberta and Saskatchewan Warriors

Manitoba Warriors.description: Indigenous street gang based in Winnipeg, Manitoba, Canada, founded in 1993 at Stony Mountain Institution to rival the Indian Posse. Engages in drug trafficking, auto theft, illegal firearms, gambling, and supports Hells Angels activities. Designated by CISC as indigenous-based organized crime. Active with approximately 300 members.

descriptions: Feud between Manitoba Warriors and Hells Angels associates over drug and prostitution rackets.
edges.evidence_quote: In August 1996, three men were shot dead inside a home in West Kildonan, Winnipeg as part of a feud over control of drug and prostitution rackets between members of the Manitoba Warriors and associates of the Hells Angels.

descriptions: Manitoba Warriors is listed as a rival of Indian Posse in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Hells Angels MC, Manitoba Warriors, Redd Alert, White Boy Posse

descriptions: The two gangs fought each other in a prison riot.
edges.evidence_quote: On 25 April 1996, a riot broke out at Headingley Correctional Institution between the imprisoned Indian Posse members versus the imprisoned members of the rival Manitoba Warriors

descriptions: Indian Posse and Manitoba Warriors are rival gangs.
edges.evidence_quote: rivalries between First Nations gangs such as the Indian Posse, the Native Syndicate, and the Manitoba Warriors

descriptions: Alberta Warriors adopted the Warriors banner from Manitoba Warriors.
edges.evidence_quote: Other organizations that have adopted the 'Warriors' banner such as the Alberta and Saskatchewan Warriors

descriptions: The Manitoba Warriors purchase drugs from the Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: The Warriors have long purchased their drugs from the Hells Angels

descriptions: Manitoba Warriors support and facilitate criminal activities for Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: CISC asserts that the gang... also supports and facilitates criminal activities for the Hells Angels motorcycle gang

descriptions: Manitoba Warriors supports and facilitates criminal activities for Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: CISC asserts that the gang, in addition to engaging in marijuana cultivation, auto theft, illegal firearms activities, gambling, and drug trafficking, also supports and facilitates criminal activities for the Hells Angels motorcycle gang and Asian-based networks.

descriptions: Manitoba Warriors were founded to rival Indian Posse.
edges.evidence_quote: the gang is an exclusively indigenous Canadian organization that was established on the Lake Manitoba First Nation in 1993 to rival the Indian Posse gang.

descriptions: MOB Squad is an affiliated organization of Manitoba Warriors.
edges.evidence_quote: Saskatchewan Warriors, Alberta Warriors, CENTRAL, Loyalty Honour Silence and MOB Squad are some of their more well known affiliations.

descriptions: Redd Alert was founded as an alternative to joining the Indian Posse or Manitoba Warriors, indicating rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: The rival Redd Alert gang were founded in Alberta's prisons by First Nations prisoners who did not want to be forced to join either the Indian Posse or the Warriors.

descriptions: Redd Alert was formed as an alternative to joining Manitoba Warriors, indicating a non-cooperative, possibly rivalrous origin.
edges.evidence_quote: Redd Alert was established in 1999 by Robert Lee Wagner along with other First Nations inmates of Cree, Anishinaabe, and Métis origin as an alternative to joining two of the other Aboriginal gangs within the Alberta prison system: the Indian Posse and the Manitoba Warriors.

descriptions: Rock Machine formed an alliance with Manitoba Warriors in 2008.
edges.evidence_quote: A Native-Canadian gang based in the Canada's prairies, an alliance was formed between the two groups during Rock Machine's expansion into Western Canada in 2008.

descriptions: Infobox lists Manitoba Warriors as a rival.

Los Metros.description: Los Metros is a Mexican criminal organization and a splinter faction of the Gulf Cartel, based in Reynosa, Tamaulipas. It was founded around 2005 by Samuel Flores Borrego, also known as 'Metro 3' or 'M3'. The group is one of five factions that emerged from the Gulf Cartel's internal fragmentation and is involved in drug trafficking, homicide, money laundering, and other crimes. It remains active and is allied with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel and the Gulf Cartel.

descriptions: Infobox lists Los Metros as an ally of CJNG.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Cártel de Tijuana, Cártel del Golfo, Los Metros, Los Chapitos, Los Viagras, Cártel de Caborca, Fuerza Anti-Unión, Los Lobos

descriptions: Rojos and Metros are rival factions within the Gulf Cartel, engaged in infighting.
edges.evidence_quote: The infighting between the Metros and the Rojos of the Gulf cartel began in 2010

descriptions: Los Metros and Los Rojos, both factions of CDG, were in conflict with each other.
edges.evidence_quote: No obstante, estos dos grupos estuvieron confrontándose desde finales del 2011 hasta principios del 2012, y se cree que Los Metros han tomado la cabeza de la mayoría de los territorios del cártel.

descriptions: Los Metros split from Cártel del Golfo, starting an internal war.

descriptions: Los Metros is a faction of the Gulf Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: On 14 March 2025, gunmen from the Los Metros faction of the Gulf Cartel were observed operating armored vehicles in Zacatecas, reportedly in support of CJNG.

descriptions: Cártel del Golfo created Los Metros as a criminal group.

Tenth Avenue Gang.description: New York City street gang formed by Ike Marsh in 1860, later becoming the city's first train robbers. The gang committed a notable train robbery in 1868, stealing $5,000 in cash and bonds. By the early 1870s, the gang merged with the Hell's Kitchen Gang after several members were killed by railroad detectives.

descriptions: The Tenth Avenue Gang merged with the Hell's Kitchen Gang in the early 1870s.
edges.evidence_quote: by the early 1870s the gang was forced to merge with the Hell's Kitchen Gang

Bufalino crime family.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Northeastern Pennsylvania, primarily in Scranton, Wilkes-Barre, and Pittston. Originated in the 1880s with Stefano La Torre, and was long led by Russell Bufalino until his death in 1994. Engaged in labor racketeering in the coal and garment industries, and colluded with New York's Five Families. The family was active from around 1900 until 2008.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Buffalo crime family

Nakano-kai.description: The Nakano-kai was a yakuza gang based in Osaka, Japan, founded by Taro Nakano after World War II. It was known for its bellicosity and was an affiliate of the Yamaguchi-gumi until 1997, when it broke away violently. The gang disbanded after a high-profile assassination of a Yamaguchi-gumi leader led to a police crackdown.

descriptions: Nakano-kai assassinated the head of Takumi-gumi, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Takumi was shot and killed in a coffee shop ... by members of a breakaway Yamaguchi affiliate, the Nakano-kai

descriptions: Nakano-kai broke from Yamaguchi-gumi in a violent attack, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: But in July of that year, the Nakano-kai broke spectacularly from its parent in a violent attack that led to the group's disbanding.

descriptions: Nakano-kai is a breakaway affiliate of Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: members of a breakaway Yamaguchi affiliate, the Nakano-kai

descriptions: Nakano-kai was an affiliate of Yamaguchi-gumi before 1997.
edges.evidence_quote: Before 1997, the Nakano-kai had been an affiliate of the Kobe -based Yamaguchi-gumi , Japan's largest yakuza group.

descriptions: Takumi-gumi retaliated against Nakano-kai after Takumi's assassination.
edges.evidence_quote: retaliating ruthlessly against the Nakano-kai

descriptions: Members of Nakano-kai assassinated the leader of Takumi-gumi, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Takumi was kumicho of yakuza's 1000-members until his assassination in Kobe in 1997 by members of the Nakano-kai.

descriptions: Hells Angels claimed Lost Tribe to prevent Outcasts from absorbing it.
edges.evidence_quote: The dispute began when the Outcasts tried to absorb a small Hertfordshire club, the Lost Tribe, in June 1997. Concerned that such a move would make the Outcasts their equal in numbers, the Hells Angels themselves then laid claim to the Lost Tribe.

Dnipropetrovsk Mafia.description: A group of Ukrainian oligarchs, politicians, and organized crime figures, and formerly Soviet politicians, based in Dnipro (formerly Dnipropetrovsk). It emerged after the Great Purge and was firmly established in the 1950s-1960s under Leonid Brezhnev. The group wielded powerful influence in Ukraine and was involved in black market activities. It declined after Brezhnev's death and Gorbachev's rise.

descriptions: The Dnipropetrovsk Mafia and Donetsk Clan were in conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: These events precipitated the conflict between the Dnipropetrovsk Mafia, led by Kuchma, and the Donetsk Clan, led by Zvyahilsky.

'Ndrina Serpa.description: The 'Ndrina Serpa is a Mafia organization linked to the 'Ndrangheta, operating in the province of Cosenza and originating from Paola, Italy. Its activities include drug and arms trafficking, illegal waste disposal, public contract rigging, extortion, and usury. Founded by Mario Serpa, the clan has been led by various members including Nella Serpa, who took over after her brother's murder. The organization has been involved in violent feuds with rival clans.

descriptions: Serpa allied with the Perna faction.
edges.evidence_quote: Con i Perna si allearono anche gli Africano di Amantea e i Serpa di Paola
